title: Everpure monthly research roundup (30d)
executive_summary: The month points to three clear direction calls: keep simplifying the platform redesign, anchor knowledge portal positioning in support expectations, and continue the Evergreen rebrand in the higher-appeal direction without adding top-of-page complexity. Events and AI Summary are no longer broad exploration tracks — they now need one decision round each with explicit winning criteria.

surfaced_findings.title: Platform redesign baselines
surfaced_findings.finding_statement: The platform redesign is strongest when the page is easier to scan at first glance. Reducing upper-page noise improves sentiment, while dense infographic treatment too early makes the page read as meant for advanced technical leads and lowers comprehension.
surfaced_findings.proof_point: Findings suggest the cleaner direction produces a stronger positive reaction and that the top-of-page infographic is the main comprehension risk.
surfaced_findings.next_step: Run one confirmation round that compares the simpler low-noise version against the current redesign, using comprehension plus sentiment together as the release gate.

surfaced_findings.title: Knowledge portal naming & structure
surfaced_findings.finding_statement: This is primarily a naming and discoverability choice, not a broad structural rewrite. People are not strongly confused by either knowledge or support framing, but the support-led version is producing the clearer positive reaction.
surfaced_findings.proof_point: Evidence suggests expectations were roughly even between knowledge and support URL options, while sentiment ran about 10 points higher for the support version.
surfaced_findings.next_step: Keep the next pass anchored in support-led naming and search expectations, then test whether the broader portal model can expand without losing that clarity advantage.

surfaced_findings.title: Evergreen rebrand direction
surfaced_findings.finding_statement: The Evergreen rebrand is strongest when it increases appeal without adding cognitive load too early. Clearer visual pacing helps, while dense infographic treatment near the top hurts both understanding and sentiment.
surfaced_findings.proof_point: Evidence suggests a color break after the hero increases appeal and sentiment, while adding more infographic content at the top reduces comprehension.
surfaced_findings.next_step: Continue with the higher-appeal direction, keep the upper page simpler, and delay denser explanatory content until after the offer is understood.

comparison_tests.title: Events page baseline
comparison_tests.finding_statement: Events-page work has moved out of broad exploration and into a narrower comparison problem. The open question is which version gives the clearest path into the event content.
comparison_tests.decision_criteria: Choose on first-glance comprehension of the page offer and clarity of the primary next step — not stylistic preference alone.
comparison_tests.next_step: Reduce to one decisive V1-versus-V2 comparison and agree on comprehension and progression criteria before the test starts.

comparison_tests.title: AI summary variations
comparison_tests.finding_statement: The AI Summary work now needs selection pressure, not more expansion. The job of the next round is to choose the single framing that is easiest to understand and easiest to trust.
comparison_tests.decision_criteria: Pick the winning framing based on which version is clearest and most credible at first read, and which makes people most confident about what the AI summary is doing.
comparison_tests.next_step: Collapse to the two strongest framings and use the next round to choose one on comprehension and confidence — not novelty.

unresolved_questions.title: Design & UX feedback
unresolved_questions.question: Which changes materially improve clarity and progression, rather than simply making the design feel cleaner in review?

unresolved_questions.title: Support taxonomy
unresolved_questions.question: Which label and path model makes support intent obvious without expanding IA complexity or fragmenting the navigation model?

unresolved_questions.title: Pathfinder CTA labels
unresolved_questions.question: Which label set is clearest at the moment of commitment and least likely to add friction at the point of action?

next_actions: Validate the simpler platform redesign against the current redesign using comprehension and sentiment together as the release gate.
Keep knowledge portal testing anchored in support-led naming and search expectations before broadening the portal model.
Continue Evergreen in the higher-appeal direction, but hold top-of-page infographic density until after the offer is understood.
Run decisive comparison rounds for Events and AI Summary with explicit winning criteria defined before the test starts.
note: This brief is a current-cycle, content-first stage-2 rendering written over the deterministic evidence layer. It is intended to support prioritization and iteration planning more than broad rollout approval.
